item.author: gabrielgcj

item.title: React Native 中的应用内聊天 | In-App Chatting in React Native
item.description:
赞恩艾哈迈德

发表于 1 月 3 日

我们经常在大多数移动应用程序中使用聊天功能，无论是与银行应用程序、电子商务和/或聊天应用程序相关。 它需要足够的时间来为不同技术堆栈中的任何应用程序构建聊天功能，“如果我告诉你一种集成/构建应用程序内聊天功能的最简单方法，该功能需要将近 15 分钟才能构建且可靠。

应用内聊天就是其中之一，我必须说，在我 4 年的经验中，我发现该平台的大部分功能都很容易采用。

在选择任何库以集成应用内聊天功能之前，您应该了解以下几点。

超低延迟

您是否曾因法律延迟而错过重要消息？ 在 ZEGOCLOUD 中，您无需担心，因为它以低至 200 毫秒的超低延迟向用户传送实时消息。

海量并发

主要问题之一是当计数为数百万时如何处理并发消息，ZEGOCLOUD 可以很好地处理它作为大规模和高要求的实时通信事件的需要

说明/步骤

  • 去ZEGOCLOUD注册一个账号
  • 转到仪表板并创建一个APP
  • 选择应用内聊天功能
  • 从仪表板转到服务管理选项，然后选择应用内聊天功能
  • 激活它
  • 复制并保存 AppID 以备后用
  • 运行命令npm i zego-zim-react-native来安装库。
  • 在组件中导入 SDK import ZIM from 'zego-zim-react-native';
  • 使用方法 create 创建 ZIM 实例，您需要AppID ** 和appSign ，您可以从仪表板获取
  • 我已经为以后的用户定义了一些状态
  • 创建一个登录功能，将用户登录到应用程序
  • 创建有助于向其他用户发送消息的 sendMessage 函数
  • 定义了一个useEffect来处理一些实时事件，如果用户连接状态发生变化，事件connectionStateChanged将通知我们，如果发生任何错误，事件错误将通知我们，如果用户从其他用户那里收到任何消息，事件receivePeerMessage将从我们可以在 UI 上显示它的地方
  • 我们还有一个注销和销毁方法，当用户离开屏幕并销毁 ZIM 实例时，这将有助于注销用户。 这是我的组件
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; import { Button, TextInput, View, Text } from 'react-native'; import ZIM from 'zego-zim-react-native'; ZIM.create({ appID: ******, appSign: '***********************' }) const zim = ZIM.geInstance(); const VideoCalling = (props) => { // const zim = ZIM.geInstance(); useEffect(() => { try { // ZIM.caller({ appID: '177503827', appSign: '4813ca648e686180b2d6e12956bf6ba8465112251090af88228754356c3939b1' }); } catch (error) { console.log(error) } }, []) const [userInfo, setUserInfo] = useState({ userID: '', // userName: '', token: '' }) const [state, setState] = useState({ message: '', otherUserId: '' }) useEffect(() => { // Set up and listen for the callback for receiving error codes. zim.on('error', function (zim, errorInfo) { console.log('error', errorInfo.code, errorInfo.message); }); // Set up and listen for the callback for connection status changes. zim.on('connectionStateChanged', function (zim, { state, event, extendedData }) { console.log('connectionStateChanged', state, event, extendedData); }); // Set up and listen for the callback for receiving one-to-one messages. zim.on('receivePeerMessage', function (zim, { messageList, fromConversationID }) { console.log('receivePeerMessage', messageList, fromConversationID); }); return () => { zim.logout(); zim.destroy(); } }, []) const handleLogin = () => { try { zim.login(userInfo, token) .then(function () { // Login successful. }) .catch(function (err) { // Login failed. }); } catch (error) { } } const sendMessage = () => { try { const messageTextObj = { type: 1, message: state.message }; zim.sendMessage(messageTextObj, toUserID) .then(function ({ message }) { // Message sent successfully. }) .catch(function (err) { // Failed to send the message. }); } catch (error) { } } const onStateHandleChange = (key, value) => { setState({ ...state, [key]: value }) } const onUserObjHandleChange = (key, value) => { setUserInfo({ ...state, [key]: value }) } return ( <View style={{ padding: 5 }}> <View> <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}> <Text>UserID</Text> <TextInput value={userInfo.userID} onChangeText={(value) => onUserObjHandleChange('userID', value)} style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} /> </View> <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}> <Text>User Name</Text> <TextInput value={userInfo.userName} onChangeText={(value) => onUserObjHandleChange('userName', value)} style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} /> </View> <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}> <Text>Token</Text> <TextInput value={userInfo.token} onChangeText={(value) => onUserObjHandleChange('token', value)} style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} /> </View> <Button title="Login" onPress={handleLogin} /> <View> <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}> <Text>Other person ID</Text> <TextInput value={state.otherUserId} onChangeText={(value) => onStateHandleChange('otherUserId', value)} style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} /> </View> <Button title="Submit" /> </View> <View> <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}> <Text>Message</Text> <TextInput value={state.message} onChangeText={(value) => onStateHandleChange('message', value)} style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} /> </View> <Button title="Send" onPress={sendMessage} /> </View> </View> </View> ); } export default VideoCalling;

链接：

示例代码库



作为 JavaScript 开发者，我们通常不必自己处理编译器。 然而，了解 JavaScript 引擎的基础知识并了解它如何处理我们对人类友好的 JS 代码并将其转化为机器可以理解的代码绝对是一件好事！ 编码愉快！

一旦暂停，zainbinfurqan 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，zainbinfurqan 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，zainbinfurqan 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 zainbinfurqan 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 zainbinfurqan 的方法：

降低 zainbinfurqan 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 zainbinfurqan 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Zain Ahmed

Posted on Jan 3

We often use a feature like chatting in most of the Mobile Application whether is related to banking app, Ecommerce and/or chatting apps. Its required enough time to build a chatting feature for any App in different Tech Stack, "What if i tell you a easiest way to integrate/build In App Chatting feature which required almost 15 mins to build and reliable.

ZEGOCLOUD is a library which offer may feature like Audio/Video calling, Live Streaming, Super Board, Cloud recording and much more.
In-App Chatting is one of them and I must say in my 4 years of experience I found the the platform easy to adopt in most of the features.

Here are some points which you should know before choosing any library to integrate In-App Chatting feature.

Ultra-low latency

Have you ever miss important messages due to law latency? well in ZEGOCLOUD you don't need to worry about is as t delivers real-time messages to users with an ultra-low latency as low as 200 ms.

Massive concurrency

One of the main issue is how to handle concurrent messages when the count is in millions, well ZEGOCLOUD handle it pretty perfectly as needs of large-scale and highly demanding live communication events

Instruction/Steps

  • Go to ZEGOCLOUD and make a account
  • Go to dashboard and create a APP
  • Select In-app chatting feature
  • Go to Service Management option from dashboard and select In-App chatting feature
  • Activate it
  • Copy and save AppID for later use
  • Run command npm i zego-zim-react-native to install library.
  • Import SDK in Component import ZIM from 'zego-zim-react-native';
  • Create a instance of ZIM using method create, You need AppID **and appSign which you can get from dashboard
  • I have define some state for later user
  • Create a login function that will login user to application
  • Create sendMessage function that will help to send message to other user
  • A useEffect is define to handle some realtime events, If user connection state change the event connectionStateChanged will notify us, If any error occur the event error will notify us & if user get any message from other user the event receivePeerMessage will have that received message from where we can show it on UI
  • We also have a logout & destroy method which will help to logout user when user leave screen and destroy the ZIM instance. Here is my component 
import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
import { Button, TextInput, View, Text } from 'react-native';
import ZIM from 'zego-zim-react-native';
ZIM.create({ appID: ******, appSign: '***********************' })
const zim = ZIM.geInstance();

const VideoCalling = (props) => {
    // const zim = ZIM.geInstance();
    useEffect(() => {
        try {
            // ZIM.caller({ appID: '177503827', appSign: '4813ca648e686180b2d6e12956bf6ba8465112251090af88228754356c3939b1' });
        } catch (error) {
            console.log(error)
        }
    }, [])
    const [userInfo, setUserInfo] = useState({
        userID: '', //
        userName: '',
        token: ''
    })
    const [state, setState] = useState({
        message: '',
        otherUserId: ''
    })

    useEffect(() => {
        // Set up and listen for the callback for receiving error codes.  
        zim.on('error', function (zim, errorInfo) {
            console.log('error', errorInfo.code, errorInfo.message);
        });

        // Set up and listen for the callback for connection status changes. 
        zim.on('connectionStateChanged', function (zim, { state, event, extendedData }) {
            console.log('connectionStateChanged', state, event, extendedData);
        });

        // Set up and listen for the callback for receiving one-to-one messages. 
        zim.on('receivePeerMessage', function (zim, { messageList, fromConversationID }) {
            console.log('receivePeerMessage', messageList, fromConversationID);
        });

        return () => {
            zim.logout();
            zim.destroy();
        }
    }, [])

    const handleLogin = () => {
        try {
            zim.login(userInfo, token)
                .then(function () {
                    // Login successful.
                })
                .catch(function (err) {
                    // Login failed.
                });
        } catch (error) {

        }
    }

    const sendMessage = () => {
        try {
            const messageTextObj = { type: 1, message: state.message };
            zim.sendMessage(messageTextObj, toUserID)
                .then(function ({ message }) {
                    // Message sent successfully.
                })
                .catch(function (err) {
                    // Failed to send the message.
                });
        } catch (error) {

        }
    }

    const onStateHandleChange = (key, value) => {
        setState({ ...state, [key]: value })
    }
    const onUserObjHandleChange = (key, value) => {
        setUserInfo({ ...state, [key]: value })
    }

    return (
        <View style={{ padding: 5 }}>
            <View>
                <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}>
                    <Text>UserID</Text>
                    <TextInput
                        value={userInfo.userID}
                        onChangeText={(value) =>
                            onUserObjHandleChange('userID', value)}
                        style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} />
                </View>
                <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}>
                    <Text>User Name</Text>
                    <TextInput
                        value={userInfo.userName}
                        onChangeText={(value) =>
                            onUserObjHandleChange('userName', value)}
                        style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} />
                </View>
                <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}>
                    <Text>Token</Text>
                    <TextInput
                        value={userInfo.token}
                        onChangeText={(value) =>
                            onUserObjHandleChange('token', value)}
                        style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} />
                </View>
                <Button title="Login" onPress={handleLogin} />
                <View>
                    <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}>
                        <Text>Other person ID</Text>
                        <TextInput
                            value={state.otherUserId}
                            onChangeText={(value) =>
                                onStateHandleChange('otherUserId', value)}
                            style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} />
                    </View>
                    <Button title="Submit" />
                </View>
                <View>
                    <View style={{ paddingVertical: 5 }}>
                        <Text>Message</Text>
                        <TextInput
                            value={state.message}
                            onChangeText={(value) =>
                                onStateHandleChange('message', value)}
                            style={{ borderWidth: 1 }} />
                    </View>
                    <Button title="Send" onPress={sendMessage} />
                </View>
            </View>
        </View>
    );
}

export default VideoCalling;

Links:

React Native In-App chatting Doc
Sample Codebase



Classic DEV Post from 2020:

🚀⚙️ JavaScript Visualized: the JavaScript Engine

As JavaScript devs, we usually don't have to deal with compilers ourselves. However, it's definitely good to know the basics of the JavaScript engine and see how it handles our human-friendly JS code, and turns it into something machines understand! 🥳

Happy coding!

Adam Crockett - Dec 31 '22

Once suspended, zainbinfurqan will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, zainbinfurqan will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by zainbinfurqan will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If zainbinfurqan is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag zainbinfurqan:

Make all posts by zainbinfurqan less visible

zainbinfurqan consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging zainbinfurqan will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: zainbinfurqan

item.title: 无需包即可轻松创建 UUID | Easily create UUIDs without a package
item.description:
发表于 1 月 3 日

所以你想创建 UUID 但不想下载node-uuid或其他一些 UUID 生成库。 那么我想您会喜欢Web Crypto API的。

看，这个 API 带有一个名为randomUUID的方法，它生成一个随机且有效的版本 4 UUID。

例子：

 const uuid = window.crypto.randomUUID(); // returns '5ac3544a-2c5e-4428-b948-41762b123e11'

希望这对您有用。 在你问之前，浏览器支持是惊人的！

马塞洛佩特里 - 22 年 12 月 22 日

一旦暂停，kitanga_nday 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，kitanga_nday 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

取消发布后，kitanga_nday 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 kitanga_nday 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您保护 DEV 社区 👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 kitanga_nday 的方法：

降低 kitanga_nday 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 kitanga_nday 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Kitanga Nday

Posted on Jan 3

So you want to create UUIDs but you don't want to download node-uuid or some other UUID generating library. Then I think you'll like the Web Crypto API.

See, this API comes with a method called randomUUID which generates a random and valid version 4 UUID.

Example:

const uuid = window.crypto.randomUUID(); // returns '5ac3544a-2c5e-4428-b948-41762b123e11'

Hopefully, this comes in handy for you. And before you ask, browser support is amazing!

Marcelo Petry - Dec 22 '22

Once unsuspended, kitanga_nday will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by kitanga_nday will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If kitanga_nday is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag kitanga_nday:

Make all posts by kitanga_nday less visible

kitanga_nday consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging kitanga_nday will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: kitanga_nday

item.title: 从任何到未知：升级 TypeScript 打字指南 | From any to unknown: A Guide to Upgrading Your TypeScript Typing
item.description:
在 TypeScript 中， unknown类型用于表示编译时未知的值。 这与any类型相反，后者表示可以是任何类型的值，包括编译时未知的值。

使用unknown优于any的一个优点是它提供了一定程度的类型安全。 当一个值的类型为unknown时，TypeScript 编译器将要求程序员在对该值执行某些操作之前执行类型检查。 这有助于防止在运行时出现意外的类型错误。

例如，考虑以下代码：

 function printLength(x: any) { console.log(x.length); } printLength("hello"); // Output: 5 printLength([1, 2, 3]); // Output: 3 printLength({ key: "value" }); // Output: undefined

在此代码中， printLength函数接受一个类型为any的值，并尝试访问该值的 length 属性。 然而，并不是所有的值都有 length 属性，所以这段代码在传递一个对象时会产生错误：

如果我们将参数x的类型更改为unknown ，TypeScript 编译器将要求我们在访问length属性之前执行类型检查：

 function printLength(x: unknown) { if (typeof x === "string" || Array.isArray(x)) { console.log(x.length); } else { console.log("Cannot determine length"); } } printLength("hello"); // Output: 5 printLength([1, 2, 3]); // Output: 3 printLength({ key: "value" }); // Output: Cannot determine length

在printLength函数的这个修订版本中，我们在尝试访问 length 属性之前使用 typeof 运算符和Array.isArray()函数执行类型检查。 这可确保代码只会尝试访问已知具有长度属性的值，并优雅地处理其他值。

使用unknown优于any的另一个优点是它强制程序员明确说明值的类型。 这可以使代码更易于理解和维护，因为它清楚地传达了程序员的意图和对所用值类型的假设。

总之， unknown类型是 TypeScript 中用于表示编译时未知值的有用工具，并提供了any类型所不具备的类型安全性和明确性级别。 为了使代码更健壮和可维护，尽可能使用unknown而不是any通常是个好主意。



原文:

In TypeScript, the unknown type is used to represent values that are not known at compile time. This is in contrast to the any type, which represents values that can be of any type, including values that are not known at compile time.

One advantage of using unknown over any is that it provides a level of type safety. When a value is of type unknown, the TypeScript compiler will require the programmer to perform type checks before performing certain operations on that value. This helps to prevent unintended type errors at runtime.

For example, consider the following code:

function printLength(x: any) { console.log(x.length);
} printLength("hello"); // Output: 5
printLength([1, 2, 3]); // Output: 3
printLength({ key: "value" }); // Output: undefined

In this code, the printLength function takes a value of type any and attempts to access the length property on that value. However, not all values have a length property, so this code will produce an error when passed an object like:

If we change the type of the parameter x to unknown, the TypeScript compiler will require us to perform a type check before accessing the length property:

function printLength(x: unknown) { if (typeof x === "string" || Array.isArray(x)) { console.log(x.length); } else { console.log("Cannot determine length"); }
} printLength("hello"); // Output: 5
printLength([1, 2, 3]); // Output: 3
printLength({ key: "value" }); // Output: Cannot determine length

In this revised version of the printLength function, we perform a type check using the typeof operator and the Array.isArray() function before attempting to access the length property. This ensures that the code will only attempt to access the length property on values that are known to have it, and will handle other values gracefully.

Another advantage of using unknown over any is that it forces the programmer to be explicit about the type of a value. This can make the code easier to understand and maintain, as it clearly communicates the programmer's intentions and assumptions about the types of values being used.

In summary, the unknown type is a useful tool in TypeScript for representing values that are not known at compile time, and provides a level of type safety and explicitness that is not available with the any type. It is often a good idea to use unknown instead of any whenever possible in order to make the code more robust and maintainable.
item.author: lynxgsm
